entry.title: Designing with Code - Update to Version 2 of UI5 Web Components
entry.content:
Designing with Code – Why Should I?

In my article series Designing with Code, I shared my experience of spending six months working within a product design team. By stepping out of my comfort zone—my beloved design tool—and diving into creating first prototypes using UI5 web components, I found myself much more connected to the actual end result. Using my entry-level coding skills, I gained insights into what happens behind the pixels, which greatly improved my discussions with developers.

In subsequent articles, I introduced how designers can leverage web components, CSS, JavaScript, and HTML to create interactive prototypes. This hands-on approach not only deepens your understanding of components but also allows you to experience how they behave in real-world scenarios.

To clarify: I am not a front-end developer. But just as a basic understanding of design benefits developers, designers can work more efficiently by grasping the basics of coding. This is particularly helpful when exploring how components interact or how screen readers interpret your designs. For me, it was a true eye-opener.

I hope this inspires you to get started! You might want to revisit Part 3, where I cover a simple prototyping setup, or Part 4, which introduces the model-view-controller pattern.

An Update: Why Some Examples No Longer Work

Unfortunately, the examples in my previous articles are now outdated due to two major changes:

  • Updated UI5 Documentation: The UI5 team migrated their documentation to a new system, integrating a sleek playground feature. This allows you to view live example code, make modifications, and preview results directly in the browser. If you haven’t tried it yet, I highly recommend it! However, this migration rendered the old links to the UI5 web components library invalid, breaking the examples in my articles.
  • Release of UI5 Web Components Version 2: A new version of UI5 web components introduced several improvements, including refactoring and stabilization, as well as exciting new components.

Given these changes, I’ve updated the examples in the GitHub repository to align with the latest version:

GitHub Repository – Designing with Code

Disclaimer: The prototyping setup described here is not intended for production use. For building production-grade applications, please refer to the official UI5 documentation.

Simple Prototyping Setup

Building production-ready applications often involves complex tools like build systems, testing frameworks, and dependency management. For designers, this can be overwhelming and time-consuming. Thankfully, there are simpler alternatives for prototyping:

Playground

The UI5 playground allows you to experiment with components by editing code and seeing instant results. This is sufficient for most exploratory tasks. However, the playground doesn’t allow for permanent saving or advanced interactions.

Local Prototyping Setup

For more complex prototypes or continuous work, a local setup offers greater flexibility. Below, I outline a minimal installation and configuration process that avoids heavy tools while providing key advantages:

  • No need to manage tools or packages locally.
  • No build steps—simply refresh your browser.
  • Easy sharing of prototypes.

Setting Up the Prototype: Key Changes in the New Approach

Import Map vs. Bundle

Previously, we imported a single bundle that included all libraries, making every component available but at a performance cost. The new approach uses an import map, which loads only the components you explicitly reference, improving efficiency and compatibility with real deployments.

To include a component, locate the <script> tag in the UI5 playground under the expanded code header. Copy and paste it into your HTML file.

I recommend using the sources available on the GitHub repository to get started. These files might also serve you as basis for your own projects going forward. The previous examples are still available. I have added an updated version in parallel folders:

  • Old Setup: Part-03-SAP-Web-Components/01_button
  • New Setup: Part-03-SAP-Web-Components 2.0/01_button

Manual Imports

With the new setup, you must manually import each required component. Create a main.js file in the same folder as your index.html and add the relevant imports. For example, to use a button component:

 

 

import "@ui5/webcomponents/dist/Button.js";

 

 

If a component doesn’t render as expected, double-check your imports.

The import statement, that is needed to import a component can be found in the documentation under the section “ES6 Module Import”. Figure 3 shows the import statement in the documentation for the Avatar component.

Running the Prototype: HTTP Server Required

Modern browsers require HTTP servers to dynamically load resources. You can use:

Visual Studio Code with Live Preview

Install the Live Preview extension for an integrated HTTP server in Visual Studio Code.

(Recommended for simplicity)

Node.js and http-server

http-server is a lightweight alternative for more control over your setup.

Is It Worth the Effort?

Admittedly, this new setup requires more effort compared to the old bundle-based approach. However, it offers significant advantages:

  1. Lightweight and Portable: Avoid managing local installations while benefiting from version control. Share your prototypes easily using platforms like GitHub Pages.
  2. Realistic Testing: Standalone prototypes allow you to test interactions like keyboard behavior, screen reader support, and resizing without playground constraints.
  3. Developer-Ready Code: The new setup aligns more closely with production code, enabling developers to reuse your prototypes effectively.

For quick experiments, the playground remains the fastest option. But for long-term projects or detailed explorations, this setup strikes a great balance between simplicity and functionality.

entry.title: My Home in SAP S/4HANA - A Deep Dive as of 2408
entry.content:
My Home is your personalized entry page within SAP S/4HANA,  that provides you with easy access to the business insights, tasks, and applications you need to do your job with greater speed and agility. My Home lets you access and manage your to-dos, your favorite pages, your favorite apps, and your insights on any device. It builds on the functionality of the SAP Fiori launchpad and leverages Spaces and Pages (as a prerequisite). The new My Home was first released with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2208 release. Since its first release, there have been great improvements in its functionality and user experience. In this blog, we’ll revisit the features of My Home that will enhance your user experience and productivity.  

Video: Welcome to the My Home in SAP S/4HANA 

My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

 

 

My Home Sections 

 

The My Home entry page is divided into different sections. You can personalize the content of each section to fit your specific needs. 

 

To-Dos 

The To-Dos section provides an overview of your daily tasks. It combines your tasks from the My Inbox application and your situations from the standard and extended My Situations applications. The tasks display relevant business information about your assigned workflow items. It enables you to process your tasks without ever having to leave My Home. If Collaborative ERP is enabled on the system, you can collaborate directly with colleagues via MS Teams to resolve issues about your tasks quickly.  

Video: Managing the To-Dos Section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home To Do Section

 

 

 

News 

The News section keeps you updated on your company's latest news, or SAP S/4HANA system news. If SAP S/4HANA system news is displaying, you can shuffle through system news from different lines of business. You can manage SAP S/4HANA system news by choosing a line of business  that interests you to stay focused on relevant information. 

Video: Personalizing the News section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home News Section

 

 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home System News articles

 

 

 

Pages  

The Pages section provides you with quick access to your favorite or most used pages. You can manage the visibility of your pages and order them in a way that enhances your productivity.  

Video: Personalizing the Pages section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home Pages Section

 

 

 

Apps 

The Apps section provides easy access to your favorite, most used, recently used, and recommended applications. The Apps section improves the discoverability of your applications. The Recommended tab offers app suggestions using SAP Business AI, based on your user-specific role assignment. You can add apps to the Favorites tab from your most used, recently used, and recommended applications. You can organize and categorize your favorite applications by using the grouping, ordering and color-coding functionality so you can access them even more efficiently. 

Video: Personalizing the Apps section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Apps Section

 

 

 

Insights Tiles and Insights Cards 

The Insights section allows you to manage and monitor your company’s activities. It shows critical business information through KPIs and micro charts. This section is broken into two sections: Insights Tiles and Insights Cards. Insights cards and tiles display dynamic content at a glance. They provide quick and easy access to the applications that can provide more detailed information. Insights tiles offer analytical information, such as charts or KPIs. Insights cards are similar to Insights tiles but provide more detailed analytical information in the form of charts, tables or lists from supporting overview page-, list report-, and analytical list page-based applications. Thanks to this information, you can assess what is happening at any time and take action when needed.  You can customize the Insights section to display information that enables you to do your job with speed and agility. 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Tiles Section 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Cards Section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Insights Section

 

 

The My Home Layout and Advanced Settings 

 You can personalize the individual My Home sections, and another key feature is the ability to customize the layout of these sections on My Home. This personalization capability allows you to prioritize the information that is most important to you. You can manage the visibility and the display order of each of the My Home sections. 

Video: Personalizing the Layout of My Home  

Once you have personalized the My Home sections you can easily share your personalized content with co-workers who have the same or a similar role as you. This  enables them to also work with speed and agility. 

Video: Sharing the Personalized Content 

 

 

 

Now that we have given you a recap of the available My Home functionality in SAP S/4HANA, we hope you enjoy working with My Home in SAP S/4HANA and reap its benefits. You can  look forward to more enhancements that will improve your My Home experience even further in future releases of SAP S/4HANA.  

Note: Innovations appear first in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The demos in the videos were recorded on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in September 2024. 

Stay tuned! 

Caroline Welsh and Anna Freitag 

Product Managers of My Home, Cloud ERP UX Engineering.

entry.title: End-to-End Guide to Consume RAP application to Fiori Using SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
To consume a RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) application in SAP Fiori using SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), you will typically follow a series of steps involving the creation of the RAP service, exposing it as a Cloud RAP service, and integrating it into a Fiori application. Below is an end-to-end guide outlining these steps. 

 Prerequisites 

Create one RAP application (Managed/Unmanaged/Unmanaged & Save). 

Step-by-Step Guide 

 Login to your BTP account, check if there is any destination, if not Create a destination to Cloud RAP   service. 

 Login to BTP account, Click on SAP Business Application Studio (BAS). 

  Provide the credentials (BTP Credentials), Click on Continue. 

 Click on OK Create Dev Space. 

 Click on New Project from Template. 

  Select SAP Fiori generator template 

  Select the template, and click on Next 

  Select the Data source which you want to create Destination. 

  Select the System (ABAP environment on SAP BTP). 

  Here there is no ABAP Environments, it requires credentials to do this follow the bellow steps. 

  Click three dots (bellow left side), select Cloud Foundry. 

  Click on Services, and login required,  

  Select the Credentials radio button and provide your username (your BTP account mail) and Password. 

 Go back and check the destination. 

  Provide Cloud foundry Details 

  Select Cloud foundry Organization (System generated) 

  Select your Cloud foundry space. 

  Organization and space have been set successfully. 

  Refresh the page. 

 Now select the ABAP Environment and your RAP Service, then Click on Next. 

 Select the Main entity and Navigation Entity and table type as responsive, then click on next. 

 Fill in the required fields, click on finish. 

 Right click on webapp, below the folder. Click on preview application. 

 Select the start Fiori run test. 

  Perform modify operations. 

 

Conclusion 

By following this guide, you will have created and consumed a RAP service using SAP Business Application Studio for a Fiori application. This approach leverages the power of SAP Fiori’s UI5 framework, combined with the RAP model for backend development, making it possible to efficiently create modern SAP Fiori apps.

entry.title: Understanding SAP Fiori Architecture: A Guide to its Three Key Layers
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Fiori is a modern UX framework designed to simplify and streamline user interactions with SAP systems. It offers a consistent and intuitive user experience across devices, ensuring that users can seamlessly access critical business applications from any platform. At the core of Fiori have a robust architecture that ensures reliable performance and seamless integration with SAP’s backend systems. In this blog, we'll take a closer look at the SAP Fiori Architecture, diving into its three primary layers: the Client Layer, Application Layer, and Database Layer. Each layer has distinct responsibilities and contributes to the smooth functioning of Fiori applications, enabling businesses to unlock the full potential of SAP's ecosystem.

The entire SAP Fiori Architecture is divided in three layers:

  • Client Layer which consists of the SAP Fiori Launchpad  and SAP UI5 App. For e.g.: a launchpad for various SAP UI5 application
  • Application Layer which consists of mainly OData services which is essential for communication between UI and other backend system such as S4/HANA system
  • Database Layer to store all the data given by the user or system. An example of the database layer is SAP HANA

We can focus on each layer and learn about various components provided by SAP Fiori Architecture as follows:

 

Client Layer:

As mentioned, before its purpose is to provide an interface where the user can interact with the Fiori apps. Following are the components in it.

  • Client Devices: These are the devices from which our SAP Fiori apps will be accessed. These could be smartphones, tablets or desktop browsers. We need to make sure that our app is responsive across all the platforms and can be used without any prior installation.
  • SAP Fiori Launchpad: The SAP Fiori Launchpad, typically hosted on SAP Business Technology Platform, acts as a centralized hub for all Fiori applications. Based on the roles configured in BTP, users can access various applications through this launchpad, providing a streamlined entry point for all SAP Fiori applications

 



Application Layer:

Its main purpose is to process user request, host UI components and manager varies services and communicate with the database layer

  • SAP Fiori Front-end Server: It hosts the UI components that are needed to run our SAP UI5 apps. Apart from that it also helps with other process such as authentication, routing and caching for faster access to application.
  • SAP Gateway: It allows the connection between the front-end and back-end by exposing various OData services. ODATA is the standard way to expose backend data to our Fiori application
  • SAP Fiori Apps Repository: It is a central Fiori repository which stores and manages all the FIORI application on SAP BTP cloud and provides across for cloud deployment. There is multiple pre-configured Fiori application available on it from SAP
  • SAP BTP: It is a management service which allows access to all the services for maintenance of roles, development spaces, various services that are used across the SAP eco-system.
  • SAP Web Dispatcher and Cloud Connector: Cloud Connector or dispatcher helps us to connect with various on-premises system without exposing the port to internet. It acts as reverse proxy which secures the to and from of data from Fiori application and backend systems
  • SAP BAS: It’s a development studio which can be used to extend standard fiori application or for development of custom SAP UI5 application for certain requirements. It comes as subscription in SAP BTP.

 

Database Layer:

The main purpose of this layer as explained before is to store, maintain and process business data which can be accessed via the application layer.

  • Database: It is used to store business data for e.g.: various sales data, records, etc. which will be accessed by the user. E.g. of such system is SAP HANA.
  • Back-end Systems: This is the core SAP system which contains the main business logic such as the amount and type of data that needs to be exposed for user via the UI5 app. The OData services are created to communicate between back-end systems and Fiori application. E.g. of such system is SAP S/4HANA.

 

 

Conclusion:

SAP Fiori’s layered architecture is pivotal for its flexibility, security, and efficiency. By distributing responsibilities across the Client, Application, and Database layers, Fiori provides a modular and scalable framework that aligns with the dynamic needs of businesses today. This architecture not only supports responsive design and seamless integration but also promotes the secure handling of data and resources. With SAP Fiori, companies can ensure a cohesive and optimized user experience across devices, empowering users to interact with complex SAP data in an intuitive and productive way. As Fiori continues to evolve, it stands to transform the way organizations interact with SAP, fostering a more accessible and efficient digital workspace.

entry.title: Essential Resources for SAP Fiori Development
entry.content:
Essential Resources for SAP Fiori Development

Hello everyone, as an SAP Fiori developer, I like to stay informed of the latest features, be technically at ease, follow development and user interface best practices, or find help when I get stuck on a problem. I'm sharing below a list of resources and websites I use daily to help me master SAP Fiori development and stay up to date (feel free to add to it in the comments with your recommendations 😉 ).

SAP Official Resources

No journey into SAP Fiori development is complete without exploring the wealth of official resources SAP provides. These resources ensure you get accurate, up-to-date information on the platform’s tools, techniques, and methodologies.

SAP Fiori Design Guidelines

This official guide covers SAP Fiori UX principles, including visual design, interaction patterns, and branding guidelines. It's crucial for developers to understand the Fiori design philosophy and ensure that the apps they develop align with SAP’s standards. These guidelines are updated regularly, reflecting evolving best practices and new SAP Fiori features.

SAP Developer Center

A central hub for all things related to SAP development. It hosts a variety of tutorials, blogs, sample projects, and developer tools with examples for novice, intermediate, and advanced developers.

SAPUI5 SDK

The documentation of SAPUI5, the framework behind SAP Fiori apps. It provides detailed API documentation, code samples, and interactive tutorials to help developers understand and use SAPUI5 effectively. The SDK also includes best practices, demo applications...

SAP Community

As one of the largest communities of SAP professionals, it is filled with interactive content including developer blogs and insights, forums for troubleshooting and peer support, SAP Fiori-focused events and webinars… You can search the community to find solutions to common development problems, get advice on the latest trends, and engage with other developers who share their experiences and knowledge.

For those looking for fun and community-driven engagement, SAP Developer Challenges are a great way to test your skills while competing with fellow developers. 

SAP Help Portal

The official documentation for all SAP products, including SAP Fiori. Whether you're working on an implementation, configuring an app, or troubleshooting issues, the Help Portal has comprehensive guidelines, API documentation, and step-by-step instructions. 

SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

This library catalogs all standard  Fiori apps, offering detailed descriptions, implementation prerequisites, and links to relevant documentation. You can also find UI5 versions and determine which apps are customizable or extensible to meet your specific needs.

Online Learning Platforms

As Fiori development is a specialized field, self-paced learning platforms can provide structured paths to accelerate your expertise. Here are some of the best online resources for learning SAP Fiori and SAPUI5:

SAP Learning Hub

For more comprehensive learning and certification preparation, it is an excellent platform that provides access to vast training content. Whether you’re a beginner or a seasoned developer, the platform offers structured learning journeys tailored to specific roles and technologies. In addition to courses, you get access to workshops, certification preparation materials, and practice exams.

SAP Certification Hub

Earning an official SAP certification is a great way to demonstrate your proficiency as a developer. As a Fiori developer, you can't miss out on SAP Certified Development Associate - SAP Fiori Application Developer certification (which I'm preparing right now 😁) By achieving certification, you prove your expertise and increase your marketability in the SAP ecosystem.

Other Fun Communities and Learning Resources

Here are some more fun and interactive resources for your learning journey:

  • Stack Overflow - SAP Fiori: Find answers to specific development challenges related to SAP Fiori, with detailed discussions on common issues, troubleshooting tips, and optimization strategies.
  • Reddit - r/SAP: Informal discussions, news, and updates on SAP technologies, including Fiori development.
  • SAP Fiori YouTube Channels: Explore YouTube channels like DJ Adams one to see live coding sessions, tips, and tricks for working with SAP Fiori, UI5, and SAP Cloud Platform. His videos are an excellent way to stay updated with the latest SAP trends, and they provide practical demonstrations of development techniques.

Conclusion

I hope this short list will help you find ways to improve your SAP Fiori skills and answer any questions you may have. Don't hesitate to share your resources, tips and experiences with the community in the comments, and above all... stay curious! 👀🚀

entry.title: SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg focused on using AI to make apps easier to build and use
entry.content:
SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg was a great success. It was our third and final event in 2024, held on November 19 in our AppHaus in Heidelberg. The goal was to empower developers, architects, and technical consultants with knowledge on how to build SAP Fiori apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and all the new AI functionality that we are building into the SAP Fiori elements framework.

 

We started by asking the attendees to define SAP Fiori. Here’s what they came up with:

 

Our customers seem to be aligned with us on what SAP Fiori means.

[ALT TEXT: A word cloud created by the attendees with the prompt “what is SAP Fiori?” The biggest words in the cloud are innovation, UX, user experience, design, and modern.]

 

It was very gratifying to see the results, as the participants have the same view of SAP Fiori that SAP does – a design system for creating modern and innovative user experiences.

 

We then launched into the day, showing examples of the SAP Fiori user experience across SAP S/4HANA, including many of the innovations that we have recently added to our public cloud version. The day proceeded as follows:

 

[ALT TEXT: graphic of the schedule for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg]

 

SAP Fiori is the face of SAP S/4HANA Cloud

The starting point for SAP S/4HANA Cloud is My Home. It provides an easy way to find and launch all your SAP Fiori apps. Most of those apps will be based on SAP Fiori elements. My Home includes a new “recommended” tab that uses AI to recommend apps to a user based on their role in the system. This makes it faster and easier for someone to get started with SAP S/4HANA.

 

[ALT TEXT: screen shot of My Home for SAP S/4HANA with the different tabs for apps displayed.]

 

From My Home, people launch the apps they need to complete their work. We use SAP Fiori elements to build around 80% of new apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It saves developers time on creating the user interface, drives consistency across apps, and ensures that all the enterprise functionality that people expect (accessibility, internationalization, responsiveness, security, etc.) are built into the applications.

 

[ALT TEXT: Two SAP Fiori elements apps in SAP S/4HANA, one showing a list report and the other an object page.]

 

AI powers application generation

Since the event was focused on developers, we spent lots of time showing our latest AI-driven tools for creating applications. In SAP Fiori tools 2403, we introduced the ability to generate applications from a business requirements document. At innovation day, we showed a lab preview of an application generator that uses an image of the app as the starting point. This was the foundation of the hands-on session where participants could sketch an image on paper or the white board and generate an SAP Fiori elements application from that. The AI creates the CAP service, the application, and even sample data so you can test your app. We plan to release this soon. The hands-on allowed participants to try SAP Fiori elements, the flexible programming model, SAP Fiori tools, and the planned AI capabilities in the context of creating a contract management application.

 

SAP Fiori is incorporating more AI for business users

We ended the day with some previews of planned AI functionality that will first be released into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editon.

  • Easy filter – natural language searching in SAP Fiori elements list reports
  • Smart summarization – AI-created summary of SAP Fiori elements object pages
  • Assisted error resolution – providing more context on system errors with recommendations to resolve them
  • Recap – a summary of what you missed when you were away from your SAP system
  • Joule-assisted insight card creation in My Home– ask Joule for the information you need and create a card that you can add to My Home

Some of these are currently in beta testing with selected public cloud customers.

 

It takes great teamwork to put on SAP Fiori Innovation Day

Putting on an event like this is a major undertaking with many contributors. Thank you to all the SAP presenters (in order of appearance): Katja Zoch, Sebastian Steinhauer, Thomas Reiss, Ioannis Grammatikakis, Carine Tchoutouo Djomo, Thorsten Lange, Ian Quigley; hands-on facilitators: Katrin Polloczek and Christoph Gollmick; partner presenters (also in order of appearance): Michael Englbrecht from eXXcellent solutions, Christophe Chan Hin from sovanta with Sören Schulz from Die Continetale, and Dr. Felix Aller & Christoph Ueberle from cbs Corporate Business Solutions; and panelists: Tina Butz from cbs, Christian Heinrich from sovanta, Michael Englbrecht from eXXcellent solutions, Ioannis Grammatikakis, head of UX Engineering at SAP.

 

There were also many SAP colleagues who worked behind the scenes to create all the demos and hands-on exercises. And, of course, the AppHaus team, led by Beate Riefer, who handled all the on-site logistics. Finally, a huge thanks to Sebastian Werner, who worked closely with me to bring this event to life.

 

Most of all, thank you to all the customers that attended, asked insightful questions, and engaged with us all and all the partners during the breaks and after hours.  

 

 

 

Next steps

  • Join us at an SAP Fiori Development Innovation Day in 2025 – vote on the locations where we should host the events
  • Register for the monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable call to meet other developers and architects who build SAP Fiori apps.
  • Subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter, in which we share updates on SAP Fiori elements, SAP Fiori tools, SAPUI5, and SAP Build Code every other month.  
  • Read about the other SAP Fiori Innovation Days we hosted in Dublin and Chicago.

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – November 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Welcome to our November release update! We're excited to present you many powerful new features. Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting your journey, we have something for you. This update features a range of exciting new capabilities, such as adding explanations to SQL statements for SAP HANA Cloud or an update to our Generative AI capabilities for MDK development.

Join us as we explore the details of what's new!

 

SAP HANA Cloud SQL Statement Explanation

The SAP HANA Database Explorer SQL Console in SAP Build Code offers a new feature for adding explanations to SQL statements for SAP HANA Cloud. The explanations can make it easier to understand complex or unfamiliar SQL queries.

The statement explanation feature is triggered by selecting an SQL statement in the SQL console and choosing “Explain SQL Statement using AI” from the menu.

Screenshot: Choosing “Explain SQL Statement using AI” from the Menu

The explanations are added to a copy of the original statement into the SQL console. The copy that contains the explanations is delimited by “AI generated SQL query explanation begins/ends” block comments.

Screenshot: AI Generated SQL Query Explanation

As with all AI generated content the generated comments should be reviewed and checked for validity. If the comments are useful, the original statement may be discarded or combined with the comments as needed.

 

Generative AI Capabilities for MDK

We are thrilled to announce an exciting update to our Generative AI capabilities for MDK (Mobile Development Kit) development. Previously, these capabilities were only available when a single project was opened at the folder level. We have now enhanced this functionality to support more scenarios. Our latest updates now enable you to use Generative AI features when working with multiple projects listed either in the Projects Explorer or at the workspace level.

For example, if you have multiple projects open in the Projects Explorer and want to generate a page in a specific project using the MDK page generation genie, it will now prompt you to select a service file from various service files in multiple projects and a page will be generated in that specific project. This enhancement provides greater flexibility and convenience when working with multiple projects in MDK development.

Screenshot: MDK page generation with Joule

 

Further readings

  • Announcing a New Guide: Deep Dive into SAPUI5 Apps on SAP BTP

We are excited to share a new comprehensive guide crafted by our colleague Matthias Schmalz, offering profound technical insights into SAPUI5 applications running on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). In this guide, Matthias delves into essential topics such as how to structure complex SAPUI5 applications to avoid common pitfalls, how multiple apps work with a shared reuse library and how to integrate business solutions as content providers into SAP Build Work Zone.

For further details, dive into Matthias' guide Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone and elevate your expertise in SAPUI5 development.

 

  • Explore Our Recent SAP Fiori Tools Release

For a deeper dive into the latest updates and features in SAP Fiori tools, be sure to check out the following blog post: SAP Fiori Tools 2411 Update: Enhanced Full-Stack Development, AI Integration, and More Open Source. This post provides a comprehensive overview of the new features and improvements that combine frontend and backend development, broaden AI-driven functionalities, and further the integration of open-source technologies.

 

  •  How to Upgrade Your Existing Standalone Service Plans

SAP Build Code offers different types of service plans in SAP BTP: trial, free and standard. For the initial setup, a booster helps you set up SAP Build Code for the plan you want to use. But what if you already use an existing standalone service plan and want to upgrade to one of the SAP Build Code service plans?

We created the following group: Update from Current Service Plan to Build-Code Service Plan. This group consists of tutorials that guide you step by step through the upgrade process:

  • Add SAP Build Code standard (Application) plan
  • Create an SAP Build Code Standard (Application) Subscription
  • Add build-code service plan
  • Update Service Plan

Our help documentation provides you a list of different current and target service plans and supported migration scenarios.

 

  • Webinar Series What's New in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08

Interested in discovering the latest innovations in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08? Then join our "What's New in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08" webinar series! This series is divided into seven captivating sessions, each filled with insights from over a dozen experts straight from our SAP HANA & Database Product Management team. Dive deep into key updates and features while gaining practical industry knowledge.

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: How SAP BTP Enhances Fiori Applications for Modern Workflows
entry.content:
Integrating SAP BTP Services into Fiori Applications

In this short article we’re looking at some core services provided by SAP BTP that can make our Fiori apps way better. BTP has tools that help us do things faster and make work easier. These are some of the services which my team have worked quite a lot lately and the examples shared below are just a high level explanation

With BTP, we can automate tasks (like creation of sales orders), make apps work well on mobile devices, set up notifications from other systems, and even add AI to help with predictions and smart decisions. All these services together make Fiori apps more flexible and powerful, so they actually fit what users need and save everyone a lot of hassle.

 

 

Core BTP Services for Fiori

SAP Build Process Automation (Workflow + IRPA)

SAP BTP’s Workflow service automates and manages business processes seamlessly within Fiori applications. Workflow allows for the creation of multi-step approval chains, task assignments, and process tracking. For example, an organization can implement a purchase order approval workflow within a Fiori app, where each approval or rejection step is automatically updated in real time. This eliminates manual processing and reduces turnaround time, enhancing operational efficiency.

IRPA complements workflows by automating repetitive, manual tasks like data entry or validations. For instance, an IRPA bot can process incoming invoices, validate them against purchase orders, and flag discrepancies for approval in the Fiori app.

Process Visibility offers end-to-end insights into workflows, providing transparency and analytics. Stakeholders can monitor live status, identify bottlenecks, and ensure SLAs are met, enabling proactive process management.

Business Rules provide flexibility in defining approval criteria. For example, an organization can configure dynamic rules based on factors like transaction value or department, ensuring that high-value approvals escalate automatically to senior management. This customization ensures streamlined and compliant operations.

Practical Scenario for SBPA: Using SAP SBPA for automating tenant onboarding in a real estate app.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: Tenant applies to move into a property.
  • Step 2: Workflow triggers and sends an approval request to the property manager.
  • Step 3: Property manager reviews and clicks “Approve” or “Reject” in the app.
  • Step 4: If approved, a notification goes to finance for deposit processing.
  • Step 5: Finance team processes payment and updates status.
  • Step 6: Maintenance team gets a notification to prep the apartment (cleaning, repairs, etc.).
  • Step 7: Once everything’s ready, tenant gets a welcome message with move-in date.

Benefits:

  • Saves time with automated approvals.
  • Clear process, everyone knows what’s next.
  • Reduces delays; no need to email or follow up manually.
           

Mobile Services

SAP Mobile Platform for BTP enable Fiori applications to be fully accessible on mobile devices, with features supporting multi-device compatibility and offline functionality. Users can interact with applications even without an active internet connection, with updates automatically synchronized once connectivity is restored. For example, in field operations, technicians can update service records on a Fiori app even in remote areas, ensuring that data is captured in real time and reflected across the organization when they’re back online.

 

Practical Scenario for Mobile Services: Maintenance team using a Fiori app to log repairs on-site.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: Worker opens the Fiori app on their mobile device while at the repair site.
  • Step 2: They fill out details (e.g., parts replaced, issue resolved) in the app, even without internet.
  • Step 3: App saves the data offline.
  • Step 4: Later, once back online, the app syncs the data with central records.

Benefits:

  • Workers can log repairs immediately, no waiting for internet.
  • Data syncs when there’s a connection, so no missed updates.
  • Reduces paperwork, everything’s in the app.

 

Advanced Event Mesh

SAP Event Mesh enables asynchronous communication, ensuring that Fiori applications can respond to events from various backend systems without delay. This service facilitates real-time event-driven responses, useful in scenarios like order processing or inventory updates. For example, when stock levels reach a predefined threshold, Event Mesh triggers a notification in the Fiori app, allowing users to act immediately to restock, thus preventing delay.

Practical Scenario for Event Mesh: Using Event Mesh in a Fiori app for tracking supply chain inventory.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: Warehouse hits low inventory level for an important product.
  • Step 2: Event Mesh detects this and triggers an alert.
  • Step 3: Fiori app instantly shows a notification for the purchasing team.
  • Step 4: Purchasing team checks the notification and orders more stock.

Benefits:

  • Teams can act immediately to prevent shortages.
  • No need to check inventory manually; they get alerts.
  • Keeps operations smooth by handling restocking on time.

SAP AI Core and AI Foundation

SAP AI Core provide machine learning and AI capabilities that integrate directly into Fiori applications. These services support predictive analytics and automated decision-making, helping users make data-driven decisions efficiently. A practical use case is in a sales application, where predictive analytics might forecast inventory requirements based on historical data, allowing users to prepare and manage resources proactively

 

Practical Scenario for SAP AI Core: Sales team using Fiori app with AI to manage inventory.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: AI model in Fiori app analyses past sales data and predicts future demand.
  • Step 2: Based on prediction, the app suggests optimal stock levels.
  • Step 3: Sales team sees recommended order amounts right in the app.
  • Step 4: They order the suggested amount to avoid running out or over-ordering.

Benefits:

  • Helps sales team avoid stock issues by planning ahead.
  • Reduces wasted stock, as predictions are data-driven.
  • Makes ordering decisions faster and more accurate.

Conclusion

Using BTP services really levels up our Fiori apps. We can set up automated workflows, support mobile use, get instant alerts when things change, and bring in AI to plan smarter. These features make the apps more helpful and let them handle new demands.

It makes the app experience smoother for everyone using it and helps the business run better too. With BTP, our Fiori apps aren’t just tools—they become something people can rely on every day to get things done more easily.

entry.title: Discover the SAP Fiori Launchpad
entry.content:
The SAP Fiori launchpad is the entry point for many SAP solutions, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP ABAP environment. 

As a business user, it provides you with intuitive access to the apps assigned to you based on your job role(s). Here you can find everything you need to get your on-system tasks done, on the device that best suits your task.

This user experience is not just about apps. The launchpad includes a range of useful features across all your apps so you can work smarter, such as AI (Joule, your digital assistant), human-to-human collaboration tools, app-to-app navigation, search by object ids, set your preferred defaults, notifications of urgent work, context-sensitive help, set your personal preferences on your home page, and more. 

SAP Fiori launchpad for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
*** Please note: Which apps and features are available will depend on your SAP solution and your access is controlled by your administrator(s).

Get a first impression in this video:

Key Features

SAP Fiori launchpad and its features bring the end-to-end experience of the user together, with scalable options for simple and complex roles, whether you are deploying a few apps or thousands. 

The launchpad allows business users to launch their apps on mobile and desktop devices, giving them all the relevant information they need at a glance. In addition, the launchpad provides all apps with services such as navigation, personalization, embedded support, and application configuration.

Please note that users can launch apps of different UI types, not only SAP Fiori apps but also classic UIs (GUI transactions in SAP GUI for HTML, ABAP Web Dynpro applications, Web Client UIs), and extended content such as remote content - e.g. custom-built apps on BTP,  SAP Analytics Cloud stories, etc. 

This makes the launchpad an ideal place to manage process changes over time for your business users. For example, gradually move your users from GUI transactions to SAP Fiori apps, using the launchpad layouts (spaces and pages) to highlight recommended versus old apps during transition and warn which apps will be removed soon.

Navigate with Spaces and Pages

The spaces and pages navigation menu is how you access your most important apps in a structured, business-meaningful way. The spaces represent a work area, while pages a work context bundling most used and relevant applications in sections for the business user.

Once you have started working in an app, the launchpad enables app-to-app navigation, so you don't need to go back to the launchpad to find your next app. Take a look at the navigation and check out short demos in the blog Getting Started with Spaces and Pages. 

The SAP Fiori launchpad for different products

The SAP Fiori launchpad is not a product you can buy separately, you get it together with different SAP solutions, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP ABAP environment and Integrated Business Planning. 

Finding information about the launchpad for your solution:

  • For all supported SAP solutions:
    • SAP Fiori launchpad | SAP Help Portal lists the SAP Help documentation for all solutions and all releases, with dedicated guides for users, administrators and developers.
    • For a brief structured overview of SAP Fiori launchpad, see SAP Fiori Launchpad | SAP Fiori for Web Design Guidelines. 
    • Discover the world of SAP Fiori in SAP Fiori Overview | SAP Help Portal.
  • For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the tutorial SAP Fiori Launchpad guides users on a first tour around the launchpad and the blog Getting Started with Spaces and Pages is a good starting point for further exploration.
  • For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and for SAP S/4HANA, check out the community SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA | SAP Community.
    • Technical names: In the SAP Fiori launchpad | SAP Help Portal, you find the SAP Fiori launchpad for SAP S/4HANA 2023 under the technical name SAP Fiori launchpad in ABAP platform 2023 with SAP_UI 758.
    • Deployment options: You can deploy the SAP Fiori launchpad on the same system as your S/4HANA ('embedded deployment') or on a separate server. SAP recommends embedded deployment. For technical details about the different deployment options, see SAP Fiori deployment options and SAP Fiori front-end server.  
    • Administration guide: Administrators are supported with a detailed Administration guide, covering the initial setup, configuration, launchpad content setup, integration and operations, including the launchpad support tools, 
    • Step-by-step tutorial: Administrators can follow the demo guide SAP Fiori Rapid Activation & Content Management, which is part of the series SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Demo Guides.  
  • For some other SAP cloud solutions, such as SAP BTP, ABAP environment or SAP Integrated Business Planning, the SAP Fiori launchpad also serves as product-specific entry point and is described as part of the solution, e.g. Administration and Operations in the ABAP Environment | SAP Help Portal.
  • SAP Business Suite systems are supported with SAP Fiori front-end server 2022, for technical details and deployment options, see SAP Fiori deployment options and SAP Fiori front-end server - SAP Community. 

Adaptation and Extensibility 

For both cloud and on-premise products, SAP offers extension possibilities for key users and for developers.

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: (once 2502 is available, I will add a link to the new S/4 Cloud Developer Guide | SAP Help Portal)  

    • Key users and developers can use key user adaptation to easily make common changes and make the adapted apps available to all users. With key user adaptation, it’s possible to rearrange fields, change field labels, add fields or simple logic, hide existing fields and features, or embed external content, such as maps. Unlike personalization, changes made through key user adaptation apply to all app users. You can even publish UI changes as a separate app, known as an app variant, making it available to other users. For more information, see Adapting SAP Fiori UIs at Runtime - Key User Adaptation.

    • If you need to make more complex changes, such as adding fragments to extension points or aggregations, developers can extend an application using developer adaptation. For an overview of possible adaptations, see Developer Adaptation, and for more detailed information, see Extending SAP Fiori Applications.

    • When a new custom app is required, developers can build SAP Fiori applications for an OData service and make it accessible to business users from the SAP Fiori launchpad, see UI Development.

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA:
    • Key users and developers can use key user adaptation to easily make common changes and make the adapted apps available to all users. Check out the options for key users Adapting SAP Fiori UIs at Runtime - Key User Adaptation
    • If you need to make more complex changes, such as adding fragments to extension points or aggregations, developers can extend an application using developer adaptation. For an overview of possible adaptations, see Developer Adaptation, and for more detailed information, see Extending SAP Fiori Applications.  
    • When a new custom app is required or if you want to enhance the launchpad with custom features, developers can follow the Developer Guide.
  • SAP BTP, ABAP Environment offers the developer guide SAP Fiori Applications in the ABAP Environment.  

SAP Fiori launchpad, SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start

For users that work mainly with one SAP solution, for example SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, the SAP Fiori launchpad offers a personalized entry point to the world of SAP S/4HANA. 

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, on the other hand, can serve as central entry point to more than one SAP system - and of course you can reuse launchpad content from your SAP Fiori launchpad ('automatic content federation'). For more information, see SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Community.

SAP Mobile Start brings launchpad apps and capabilities to extended device types, such as wearables (smartwatches), foldable phones and Apple Vision Pro.

entry.title: Steps to Replace OData in Business Application Studio UI5 Extension Project
entry.content:
How to change OData service when using BAS Extension Project for Standard Apps

Background: Sometimes, standard FIORI apps cannot be extended via BAS Adaptation Project. This limitation arises if the app contains synchronous views. In such cases, you may need an alternative approach to customize the app effectively. 

Requirement: The standard app ‘Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant’, includes buttons to transfer stock between plants. My requirement is to customize the app to grey out the buttons based on the plant category. 

Solution:

This standard app uses OData service MMIM_MATERIAL_DATA_SRV. To implement the required changes, I created a custom OData service ZMMIM_MATERIAL_DATA_SRV, based on the standard service with an additional field. While the creation of this service is beyond the scope of this blog, I will focus on integrating it into the app.

The buttons I customized are highlighted in the below image.

Image source: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1957')/S21OP

Steps to Implement the Customization

1. Start by opening the BAS tool and selecting the Adaptation Project option.

2. Fill in the necessary details for the project setup and proceed to the next step.

3. Select the relevant system associated with the project.

4. Upon choosing the standard app, a message will appear indicating that the app contains synchronous views.

5. Due to the limitation, you cannot proceed with an Adaptation Project. Instead, create an Extension Project and click Finish.

6. Once the Extension Project is created, open it separately (optional). Then, create an extension by right-clicking the .extconfig.json file.

7. In the context menu, choose ‘Extend Controller’ and then select ‘Copy of the Original Controller’.

8. Uncomment the function ‘_loadMaterial’ in the file ‘S1Custom.controller.js’. This is where the oData service needs to be replaced.

9. Replace references to the standard OData service with the custom OData service ZMMIM_MATERIAL_DATA_SRV in your UI5 extension project. The required changes are shown below:

10. Search for all occurrences of the model  ‘oMaterialData’ in the code. Update them accordingly to use the custom service.

11. In the custom OData service, I added a new property ‘PlantCategory’ to the existing entity type ‘MaterialPlant’.

12. Update the UI5 code to use new property ‘PlantCategory’ in the function ‘_checkFirstClickIsAllowed’. This ensures the buttons are always greyed out based on the plant category.

Testing:

Before the change (in the standard app):

                                                               

After the change (in the enhanced app):

As it can be seen from the above output, the buttons in the entire row are disabled. Requirement has been achieved.

entry.title: 🎄💻Advent of Code 2024: Exploring UI5
entry.content:
The Advent of Code (AoC) is an annual event that excites the programming SAP community every December. It's a series of daily coding challenges that run from December 1st to December 25th, offering a fun and engaging way to improve your coding skills. This year, let's dive into how you can tackle these challenges using UI5, a powerful framework for building enterprise-ready web applications.

Image source : Google Images

Getting Started with UI5

To get started with UI5 for Advent of Code, you'll need to set up your development environment. Here are the basic steps:

  1. Install Node.js and npm: UI5 requires Node.js and npm for development. You can download and install them from the official Node.js website.
  2. Set Up UI5 CLI: The UI5 Command Line Interface (CLI) is a powerful tool that helps you manage your UI5 projects. Install it using npm:

 

npm install --global @ui5/cli
npm install --global yo
npm install --global create-easy-ui5

 

This simple example demonstrates how UI5 can be used to create interactive solutions for Advent of Code challenges. By leveraging UI5's powerful features, you can build robust and visually appealing applications to solve even the most complex problems.

Github repo for AoC 2024
https://github.com/yogananda-muthaiah/adventofcode2024

SAP UI5 Components

https://ui5.sap.com/#/controls

SAP UI5 Icons Explorer

https://ui5.sap.com/test-resources/sap/m/demokit/iconExplorer/webapp/index.html

Live Editor

https://ui5.sap.com/#/liveEditor

UI5 Tooling

https://sap.github.io/ui5-tooling/stable/

UI5 Linter

https://github.com/SAP/ui5-linter

Conclusion

Advent of Code is a fantastic opportunity to sharpen your coding skills and have fun with the global programming community. Using UI5, you can enhance your solutions with rich UI components and efficient architecture. So, get ready to code, learn, and enjoy the Advent of Code 2024 with UI5!

entry.title: How to resolve "backend error occurred" in SAP GUI for HTML (WebGUI)
entry.content:
Hi Community,

This blog will help you on how to troubleshoot and resolve backend error issues in SAP GUI for HTML.

While navigating in SAP GUI for HTML or Fiori, you may face "backend error occurred" pop-up and can't proceed with your activity flow. On the contrary, the same scenario works fine with SAP GUI for Windows.

 

  • What is a backend error?

Most of backend errors are self explanatory, the "control not found" for example, means that the control does not exists in a screen when the problematic post request for the control is sent to backend.

"failed to fire action", "failed to set focus" and other errors may also be outlined in the backend error details.

Basically, these are programming errors that needs a correction from WebGUI side, most of them are already resolved in latest Kernel patches.

 

  • How to resolve the backend error?

SAP recommend you to install the latest Kernel SP available as WebGUI corrections are delivered with Kernel patches.

If you cannot update the complete kernel, it is enough to update the ITS related part (webgui corrections) of the kernel using the sapwebgui*.sar file as described in SAP Note 2412840.

 

  • Test yourself

If you have a sandbox system where you can play with, the Kernel can be updated for test purposes, just to see if the issue occurs there. You can find the step by step below:

Easiest way to test SAP GUI for HTML (WebGUI) with latest kernel patch

 

  • and if the issue persist in the Kernel latest patch?

If users are still facing the backend error in the latest Kernel patch, you should open a case with SAP Technical Support with the following information:

  • Exactly step by step to reproduce the problem
  • HTTP traces reproducing the issue (1816543 - How to create HttpWatch trace to troubleshoot ITS related problem)
  • Support communication traces as per KBA 3324689

This will help us to track the problem and provide a correction as soon as possible.

 

All the best,

Lucas Machado

entry.title: Deprecation of karma-ui5 plugin
entry.content:
SAPUI5/OpenUI5 provides several testing options, like unit and integration tests, which are summarized in UI5 Testing:

Picture 1: Testing UI5 applications

To ensure that code is tested thoroughly before it is included in a productive app, using a test runner is best practice to automate the regular execution of tests. The test runner can be included in a project setup to be called whenever code changes are submitted.

In this context, the UI5 team is now faced with a new situation: Handling current problems with browsers would require an update of the Karma test runner, which is deprecated.

The Karma test runner is deprecated since April 2023. Now we have to also deprecate karma-ui5.

(Please note that while karma-ui5 is deprecated and does not receive further updates, it can still be used as long as it fulfils your needs.)

UI5 does not provide features for a specific other test runner, but rather aims for an agnostic approach. To execute functional and integration tests you may use a test runner of your choice with the respective configuration for QUnit and OPA5 tests. One test runner alternative with the respective configuration that executes tests in parallel is the ui5-test-runner, which is listed in the UI5 community projects. For more details see this Devtoberfest 2023 session.

You might also be interested in this UI5ers live webcast which discusses test runners.

entry.title: Joule on Mobile & Start Screen Personalization: SAP Mobile Start V2.1 - Release Update
entry.content:
Just recently, Joule - our generative AI copilot turned 1!
In this one year, Joule has revolutionized the way how users interact with their business solutions, be it HR, Sales, Procurement or IT – but there was one significant piece missing: mobile access.

With our latest releases of SAP Mobile Start, you can now use Joule while being mobile. Instead of searching for the right app, opening it, and then looking for the info, you can now ask Joule in natural language.

While our teams add more and more business content/ scenarios and functions to Joule, we also plan to embed Joule into further mobile apps, like SAP SuccessFactors or SAP Service and Asset Manager.

But now, let´s have a look at how Joule within SAP Mobile Start helps you work faster, get smarter insights when you need them, and interact more smoothly with your business systems while being mobile.

The Joule mobile client is embedded within the SAP Mobile Start header bar and can be opened with a single tap from any main screen. It provides the same main functionality and content as the desktop client if the content is enabled for mobile usage.

Screenshot: SAP Mobile Start with embedded Joule

After opening the Joule client, you just enter your commands in natural language and Joule provides you with the respective answer.

Let´s have a look at three concrete examples:
Imagine you are in a meeting and need to check on your sales orders that have not been delivered so far. With Joule in SAP Mobile Start, you just ask “Show sales orders with delivery status not completed” and Joule will provide you the respective list.

 Screenshot: Joule – Example open orders (SAP S/4HANA)

Or you are a consultant who is new to the role, and a customer asks you how to customize a sales order in SAP S/4HANA. With Joule in SAP Mobile Start, you just pull out your phone and ask Joule, and you will get immediately the right SAP Help pages listed.

 Screenshot: Joule – Example documentation (SAP Help)

Finally, imagine you have a new team member, Maria, who has just joined from another team, and you want to quickly check when she has initially joined the company. Just ask Joule.

 
Screenshot: Joule – Example hiring date (SAP SuccessFactors)

Joule also allows you to navigate to other apps to get further details. Those could be web apps or native apps like the native SAP SuccessFactors app. So, if you want to learn more about Maria and her career so far, you just tap the “Profile” button and Joule will open Maria´s profile in the native SAP SuccessFactors app on your phone, with all the information about your new team member.

 Screenshot: Example native app integration

To initiate a Joule conversation, you're not limited to type in the request. You can now use Siri and Siri Shortcuts to seamlessly interact with Joule - which even works better with Apple Intelligence. Simply say "Hey Siri, ask SAP. Show my sales orders with delivery status not completed" and Joule will provide you with the respective content within the app.

Joule truly changes the way you interact with your business systems – it becomes much more natural. You can articulate your business needs in natural language and Joule responds by swiftly providing seamless access to your enablement content what will help you to execute your tasks faster and in a far more convenient way.

As of today, not all your prompts and web apps are supported on the phone; but more and more Joule business skills and scenarios are added. We are also working on adding some valuable mobile-specific Joule capabilities, so stay tuned for more.

If you have enabled Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, or SAP Start, the mobile Joule client will automatically be available in SAP Mobile Start. No additional steps are needed.

Do not miss our brand new Joule in SAP Mobile Start video: “Bring Joule Everywhere! SAP's AI Copilot is Now Available on Mobile”. 

Besides bringing Joule to mobile, we have added more helpful features to further enhance your experience with SAP Mobile Start, like Personalization of the Start Screen. With the new release, you can easily move the Start Screen sections such as Insights or News up or down as they work best for you – and hide sections if you do not need them. As always, it is super easy and intuitive and will boost your productivity as you can adjust the Start Screen now, based on your preferences.

 Screenshot: SAP Start Screen personalization

Finally, we have enhanced the Apps and To-Dos Screen. Now you can expand and collapse your spaces and pages section on the app screen, and we have provided an enhanced filtering experience on the To-Do screen that allows you to set multiple filters.

Screenshot: To-Dos Screen with filtering option

I hope you will enjoy those new features in SAP Mobile Start.

For further information on the new topics, please check our SAP Mobile Start documentation.
SAP Mobile Experience offers intelligent native mobile solutions that help businesses build more efficient, resilient and sustainable end-to-end processes, improving people’s work life wherever they are.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

entry.title: Enhancing Failure Analysis with AI-Powered Fiori App
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! Today, I’m thrilled to share a project that bridges traditional failure analysis methods with modern AI capabilities. Leveraging the power of React and UI5 Web Components, I developed a Fiori app tailored for industrial maintenance workflows. Unlike standard forms for failure analysis, this application integrates artificial intelligence to provide actionable insights and recommendations for preventing recurring issues.

In this blog post, I’ll walk you through the journey of building this application, highlighting the technologies used, the architecture, and how it addresses real-world challenges in maintenance processes.

 

preview results:

Problem Statement
In the industrial environment, failure analysis is critical to reducing downtime and ensuring efficient operations. Traditional approaches involve:

  • Documenting failures using structured forms.
  • Creating action plans (e.g., 5W2H, 5 Whys).
  • Relying on human expertise to derive insights and solutions.

While effective, these methods can be time-consuming and dependent on the analyst’s experience. This led me to explore how AI could enhance the process by:

  • Automating the generation of recommendations.
  • Standardizing responses to common problems.
  • Providing data-driven insights to augment human decision-making.

Application Overview

The app combines the familiar functionality of SAP Fiori forms with advanced AI capabilities. Here are its key features:

Core Functionalities

  1. Failure Documentation

    • Users can input detailed descriptions of failures.

    • Form fields are structured around methodologies like 5W2H.

  2. AI-Powered Suggestions

    • Integrated with an AI model (via API) that analyzes the problem.

    • Provides suggestions to prevent similar issues in the future.

  3. Action Plan Generation

    • Automatically drafts a preliminary action plan based on AI insights.

  4. History and Trends

    • Allows users to review past analyses and identify recurring patterns.

Technology Stack

Frontend

  • React: For building a dynamic and user-friendly UI.

  • UI5 Web Components: To ensure consistency with SAP Fiori design principles.

Backend

  • Node.js: For handling API requests and routing.

  • Python (Flask): Hosting the AI model and processing user inputs.

AI Integration

  • The AI model leverages natural language processing (NLP) to:

    • Understand problem descriptions.

    • Suggest actionable recommendations.

 

Development Highlights

Why React and UI5 Components?

Combining React with UI5 components allowed me to:

  • Leverage modern frontend capabilities while maintaining SAP’s design consistency.

  • Benefit from React’s flexibility and UI5’s pre-built, accessible components.

there is a very good demonstration:

https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/build-your-own-react-application-with-ui5-web-components-for-react/ev-p/283244

 

AI Model Integration

  • Trained on historical failure reports and action plans.

  • Continuously improves with feedback from users.

Overcoming Challenges

  1. Ensuring SAP Fiori Design Compliance:

    • Adapted UI5 Web Components for a native look and feel.

  2. Optimizing AI Recommendations:

    • Incorporated feedback loops to refine suggestions.

Demonstration

Here’s how the app works:

  1. Input the Problem:

    • The user describes the issue in free text and selects related categories.

  2. Generate Suggestions:

    • The AI analyzes the description and presents actionable recommendations.

  3. Review and Finalize:

    • Users review AI-generated insights and finalize the action plan.

  4. Save and Track:

    • The data is stored for future reference and trend analysis.

Fiori App

Ai solution 

 

Follow bellow one sample used in fiori solution:

 

follow the suggestion from model:

' .. 

  • Establish a comprehensive preventive maintenance schedule:
    • Define the ideal lubrication frequency for bearings based on manufacturer recommendations and operating conditions.
    • Include regular motor temperature checks and vibration measurements.
  • Continuously monitor motor temperature:
    • Install temperature sensors on bearings and other critical points.
    • Set up temperature alerts to notify the maintenance team of potential issues.
  • Train the maintenance team:
    • Provide comprehensive training on motor lubrication requirements and best maintenance practices.
    • Establish clear procedures for lubrication and monitoring.
  • Use high-quality lubricants:
    • Select lubricants appropriate for operating conditions and motor requirements.
    • Maintain a stock of lubricants and ensure their proper application.
  • Regularly analyze maintenance records:
    • Track lubrication, motor temperature, and vibration data to identify trends and predict potential problems.
    • Adjust the maintenance schedule as needed.
  • Promote a proactive mainte.nance culture:
    • Emphasize the importance of regular preventive maintenance and continuous monitoring.
    • Recognize and reward the maintenance team for excellence in maintenance and failure prevention.' 

Benefits and Impact

This application aims to:

  • Save Time: Automate the recommendation process.

  • Enhance Decision-Making: Provide data-driven insights.

  • Standardize Processes: Reduce variability in failure analysis.

Next Steps

This project is just the beginning! I plan to:

  • Enhance AI Capabilities: Improve suggestion accuracy with more data.

  • Integrate with SAP: Connect directly to SAP PM for seamless workflows.

Conclusion

Building this application has been a rewarding experience, combining SAP’s robust ecosystem with cutting-edge AI technologies. I’m excited to hear your feedback and suggestions for improvement.

Feel free to connect with me here on the SAP Community or via LinkedIn. Let’s collaborate to make failure analysis smarter and more efficient!

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/bernardo-caldas/

entry.title: SAPUI5 Meets TypeScript: A Smooth Migration Journey with Joule
entry.content:
As announced in March in this blog post by @OliverGraeff, the UI5 team has taken its first glance into the world of generative AI as a contribution to Joule in SAP Build Code. This blog post is dedicated to providing insights into the latest feature that offers AI-driven migration of UI5 applications to TypeScript.

TypeScript and UI5 – how can I use it ?

Since 2023, UI5 applications can officially be implemented in TypeScript by generating projects configured for transpiling of TypeScript or by migrating the project structure as described in this blog post by @AndreasKunz.

In addition to converting the JavaScript code to TypeScript, migrating UI5 projects to use TypeScript requires installing the required dependencies to the project and to enhance the build and server configuration with the ‘ui5-tooling-transpile-task’ and ‘ui5-tooling-transpile-middleware’.

Joule now provides AI-driven convenience functionality to migrate existing UI5 applications according to the latest recommendations on how to enable TypeScript.

Let’s migrate to TypeScript with Joule!

When using Joule in SAP Business Application Studio, there is now an additional /ui5 prefixed slash command /ui5-typescript for UI5 applications available.

While the standard /ui5 command can be used for general queries regarding UI5 development, /ui5-typescript will specifically provide options meant to migrate existing JavaScript-based UI5 applications to TypeScript. As soon as the command is selected, a list of suggestions will appear.

Migrating the project setup

If you  begin to write controller code in TypeScript, you need to migrate the existing project first to enable the required tooling.

 

What’s happening here? After sending the prompt, Joule will recognize the project structure of the application and adjust the dependencies in the package.json and configuration in the ui5.yaml. A click on the files displayed in the list will show the changes made by Joule and AI, here, for example, the changes made for package.json:

In addition to using AI, Joule will check the npm registry to retrieve the latest stable releases for the required dependencies, therefore the responses are a hybrid of AI and tooling-driven migration. The results should nevertheless always be double-checked before accepting them. For example, as shown in this screenshot, AI recommends doing a major upgrade of the UI5 CLI, which should be evaluated and tested.

The “Accept” button in the file list can be used to accept all file changes at once. This will also include necessary files, such as the newly added tsconfig.json, to configure your migrated UI5 project.

Migrating controller files

After successfully migrating the project, we recommend to migrate smaller batches of controllers to TypeScript. Again, select the /ui5-typescript command in Joule – the suggestions will provide the currently selected controller file in your project that will be migrated to TypeScript. The results can again be checked by clicking on the resulting files to view the differences.

As soon as the “Accept” button is pressed, the previous *.controller.js file will be deleted and replaced with the accepted *.controller.ts file.

You can also directly use /ui5-typescript and specifically ask for certain JavaScript files to be migrated. The recommendation is to migrate files in your application step by step to get the best possible results.

Still eager to learn more about UI5 and AI? These blog posts might be interesting for you!

  • Now AI Helps You Developing SAPUI5 Applications
  • SAP Fiori tools 2403 introduces Gen AI capability and more 
  • UI5 freestyle development in SAP Build Code with Joule

entry.title: Unlocking Efficiency: Leveraging SAP BTP Document Management Service in Fiori Applications # 2
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

In today's digital landscape,efficient document management is crucial for businesses aiming to streamline operations and enhance productivity.The SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Document Management Service offers a robust solution for managing documents seamlessly within SAP Fiori applications.
In this blog series we will explore different scenarios and find suitable solutions to integrate DMS into SAP Fiori Applications. This will help consultants to
Blog # 1: Embed SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in a Custom Fiori App  

Blog # 2: Embed SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in an SAP Fiori Elements application

Blog # 3: Using SAP BTP DMS in an SAP Business Process Automation and integrate to a Task UI application
Blog # 4: Using SAP BTP DMS API for Custom requirements

In the first part of the blog series,we covered basic features of SAP BTP DMS Service,the Reusable UI component and its integration with a Custom Fiori application.

In the 2nd installment of the series, we will cover using SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in

  • Scenario #1: SAP Fiori Elements List Report App (oData V2)
  • Scenario #2: SAP Fiori Elements List Report App (oData V4) in a CAP application

Prerequisite

  1. Set up a Dev Space in Business Application Studio
  2. Add Entitlement for following services in your subaccounta. SAP BTP Document Management Service, Integration Option – Standard (Instance)b. SAP BTP Document Management Service, Application Option – Standard (Subscription)
  3. Cloud Foundry

Setting Up Document Management Service

We have set up SAP BTP DMS in the previous blog. We have created 2 new folders for the purpose of this blog.

 

Note down the Repository ID and Folder ID (Object ID) for the “General” folder. This will be used in the current blog. If you are using DMS Application option, the Root ID and Folder ID can be retrieved form directly the url. I will be in format of
https://<sub-account-url>/cp.portal/site#user-display?sap-ui-app-id-hint=com.sap.ecm.sdm.user&/documents/v1/open/rep=<RepositoryID>&obj=<ObjectID>

You may also use DMS API to retrieve the details. Please check the link shared earlier.

SCENARIO#1

Embed SAP BTP DMS Reuse UI to Fiori Elements App based on Nothwind oData V2 service.

Step 1:

Create a new Fiori Elements Application “dmsreuseui.listreport.v2” using SAP Fiori Generator wizard in Business Application Studio.

 

Step 2:

Update Manifest file to use the Reuse UI.

Add the Component usage following under “sap.ui5” section.

 

 

"componentUsages": {
      "documentTable": {
        "name": "com.sap.ecm.reuse.documentTable"
      }
    },

 

 

After Component Usage ,add the resource roots attribute.

 

 

"resourceRoots": {
      "com.sap.ecm.reuse.documentTable": "./../../comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreusedocumentTable/",
      "com.sap.ecm.reuse.admin": "./../comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreuseadmin/"
    }

 

 

The standard Reuse UI comes in 2 variants “documentTable" and “admin”. We are going to use “documentTable” variant for this blog series.

Step 3:

Add an embedded component.

 

 

"embeddedComponents": {
     "simple:Attachment": {
                "id": "Attachment",
                "componentName": "com.sap.ecm.reuse.documentTable",
                "title": "{{Attachment}}",
                "settings": {
                  "destinationPath": "/comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreusedocumentTable/api",
                  "repositoryId": <Repository ID>,
                  "objectId": <Object ID>
        }
 }

 

 

Step 4:

Update xs-app.json file to include route for DMS Integration API.

 

 

 

{
   "source": "^/comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreusedocumentTable/api(.*)$",
   "target": "$1",
   "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
   "service":"com.sap.ecm.reuse",
   "endpoint": "ecmservice"
}

 

 

Step 5:

Update MTA file to enable authentication and authorization using client credential between Fiori Application and DMS service.

Add Document Management Service, Integration option as a module.

 

 

- name: test-dms
    parameters:
      service-key: 
        name: sdm-key

 

 

 

Add destination configuration for DMS instance using the client credentials.

 

 

- Name: test-dms
   Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
   ServiceInstanceName: test-dms
   ServiceKeyName: sdm-key

 

 

Finally, add the DMS instance under resources section

 

 

- name: test-dms
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: sdm
    service-name: test-dms
    service-plan: standard

 

 

Step 6:

Deploy your app in cloud Foundry environment. You may choose to use context menu in project or use terminal commands (mbt build & cf deploy)

Step 7:

Log-in to SAP BTP Cockpit and find the deployed application under „HTML Applications“ section.

Click on the application to launch it in a new tab.
Select an Order from list Report table and navigate to Object page

The application has the Reuse UI embedded and the "Fiori Elements"  folder is launched as Root folder.

Please note: If you face any issues in rendering of the Attachment section, try using the following code to rerender the component as workaround in Object Page extension.

SCENARIO # 2:

Embed SAP BTP DMS Reuse UI to an SAP CAP Application.

Prerequiisites:

We are going to use sample incidents CAP application. Follow the steps provided in tutorial https://developers.sap.com/group.cap-application-full-stack.html

Step1:

As the Fiori App is already available from the tutorial, we will move to next step to embed the Reuse UI.
